mission: Map the convergence surface between the Lexon computational law stack (Diedrich, lexon.org) and the 0xagentprivacy architecture (Privacy Value Model, dual-agent Swordsman/Mage, VRC/RPP, MyTerms contractual privacy paradigm). Load-bearing thesis: Lexon controlled grammar is a deterministic knowledge-graph grammar (AST = subject-predicate-object triples); contracts compile to subgraphs; gates/commitments/promises are typed edges forming promise graphs across contracts. Produce one convergence chronicle per letter, a synthesis, and three artefacts feeding (1) a working session with Diedrich, (2) the Privacy is Value register pipeline, (3) a bonfires.ai adoption note.

voice_constraints.numerics: exact or Fibonacci-anchored numbers, never round hyperbole

letters.title: The Holy Grail of Computational Law
letters.author: Henning Diedrich

letters.title: Creating Cryptolaw for the Uniform Commercial Code
letters.author: Carla L. Reyes (SMU)

letters.title: Languages for Smart and Computable Contracts
letters.author: Christopher C. Clack (UCL)

letters.title: Merging traditional contracts (or law) and (smart) e-contracts
letters.author: Florian Idelberger (EUI)

axes.name: Performative agreement
axes.map: Lexon felicitous speech-act on unstoppable substrate (Austin, fn.7 Bovill) <-> agreement ceremony; words that become true by uttering them are ceremonies that bind

axes.name: Gates as promises
axes.map: Lexon gates, commitments, promises (voluntary per-account obligations) <-> Promise Theory + polarity, VRC-as-promise-bundle; obligation expressed voluntarily, never imposed

axes.name: Topology of trust and promise graphs
axes.map: Lexon star-to-web decentralisation (Fig. 5/6) <-> bilateral VRC graph vs issuer-centric credentials; gate/commitment/promise primitives as typed-polarity edges; corpus of contracts as a promise graph spanning contract boundaries; stratum-weighted network value

axes.name: Private evidence chains
axes.map: Lexon micro blockchain (hash-chained signed logs, perfectly private state) <-> ceremony records, reconstruction ceiling R < 1, contractual privacy without public disclosure

axes.name: Grammar to graph (keystone)
axes.map: Lexon nouns as neutral markers, meaning in relations (Wittgenstein); AST as deterministic subject-predicate-object triples: readable contract, executable code, and lossless graph serialisation at once <-> RPP proverbs as opaque bilateral edges; public-legible subgraphs with opaque edges = reconstruction ceiling drawn as topology

axes.name: Accountable reversibility
axes.map: Lexon forum-key conditional reversibility <-> Accountable Wallet, Watcher Network, Privacy Pools association sets; discretion anchored to a designated neutral

axes.name: Terms as executable law
axes.map: Reyes U.C.C. model law and Lexon-as-legalese <-> MyTerms IEEE P7012 machine-readable terms; the contractual privacy paradigm needs an execution layer, Lexon is a candidate

axes.name: Anchoring below the agent
axes.map: Lexon token-layer obligations (what the substrate does not provide, the language cannot support) <-> three-axis separation, Swordsman constraints beneath the Mage; Robotic Laws as boundary inscriptions

phases.name: Letters
phases.action: For each letter: fetch (unless in_hand), read brief, produce convergence chronicle to output path

phases.name: Synthesis
phases.action: Merge five chronicles along eight axes into out/SYNTHESIS.md; verdict per axis: structural convergence, terminological convergence (false friend), or divergence

phases.name: Artefacts
phases.action: Produce out/HENNING_BRIEF.md (technical overlap brief, three collaboration probes, analysis-led), out/PVM_QUEUE.md (CTR-LEX candidate entries for register consideration; no C-series numbers, no confidence percentages), out/BONFIRES_NOTE.md (Lexon as deterministic write-path and promise-graph overlay for Bonfire knowledge graphs, with worked Lexon-to-triples example)

pvm_register.rule: Agent proposes CTR-LEX candidates only. The live conjecture register (C-series) is the single source of truth; the agent never assigns C-series IDs or confidence percentages. Every candidate carries claim, evidence pointer, falsification condition, suggested V6 claim-cluster fit.

bonfires.stack: Graphiti over Neo4j (entities, relationships, episodes), Weaviate vectors, MongoDB chunks, shared graph per Bonfire, episode-write API, automatic conversation capture
bonfires.proposal: Lexon controlled grammar as deterministic authoring/write-path alongside probabilistic extraction; promise primitives as typed promise-graph overlay; zero extraction loss for agreements, decisions, commitments
